title: Public Types, Methods and Fields (API) Should Be Documented With Javadoc
description: Public APIs without proper documentation can lead to a lack of clarity, increased development time, higher potential for errors, difficult maintenance and poor collaboration. Documenting the API using JavaDoc can clarify the contract of the API, which is particularly important for public APIs as they are used by other developers.
recommendation: It is recommended to document the API using JavaDoc to clarify what is the contract of the API. This is especially important for public APIs, as they are used by other developers.
impact: Undocumented APIs can lead to misuse, unexpected results, increased development time, higher likelihood of mistakes leading to bugs or system crashes, and difficulties with maintenance, updates and collaboration. Documenting APIs can mitigate these issues.

checks.message: It is recommended to document the API using JavaDoc to clarify what is the contract of the API. This is especially important for public APIs, as they are used by other developers.
